title: sarothamnus scoparius subsp. prostratus
intro: Sarothamnus scoparius subsp. prostratus, also known as prostrate broom or creeping broom, is a subspecies of the broom plant (Sarothamnus scoparius) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Sarothamnus scoparius subsp. prostratus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and provide antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of forms.
